scope.summary: Expose readable swarm lane/message diagnostics in UI/debug surfaces.
forbidden_paths: decorative dashboard-only work without backend state
UI text that hides lane ids, message refs, or recovery state
treating app/src legacy React/Tauri surfaces as canonical implementation authority
npm/JavaScript proof targets for this WP
app/src-tauri launch or IPC authority for this WP
proof commands that pass with zero matching tests
acceptance_criteria: Debug surface shows run id, lanes, statuses, message counts, last activity, payload errors, and orphan state.
UI can inspect a single ModelLaneMessage payload ref and promotion state.
Visual verification confirms readable layout and no overlapping text.

Debug/visual surface MUST expose trace_id, span_id/parent_span_id or span links, EventLedger event ids, Flight Recorder correlation ids, and per-behavior HBR-INT-009 posture for Flight Recorder/internal_diagnostics/Palmistry. If internal_diagnostics or Palmistry are absent in this worktree, the surface MUST show DEFERRED-with-reason and follow-up hook, not a blank/missing tier.

Diagnostics MUST show, filter, and deep-link run_id, lane_id, message_id, Locus wp_id/mt_id/task_board_id, session_id/model_session_id, context_bundle_id, memory_pack_ref/hash, artifact_ref, Loom block refs when present, Flight Recorder/EventLedger ids, promotion/validator decision refs, and recovery hints. Visual proof MUST compare rendered rows against canonical backend counts and refs.
The WP does not name or rename the internal viewing tool; it consumes the approved name when available and otherwise implements backend-equivalent navigation without blocking state recovery.
Legacy app/src swarm components may be inspected only for parity/reference; implementation authority is native Rust/backend state and native UI surfaces.

All GUI/debug implementation and proof for this MT must be Rust native egui/Argus or Rust backend TraceProjection. TypeScript, React, Tauri/WebView, and npm tests are forbidden as implementation or proof authority.

Implement a Rust-native diagnostics surface with provisional contract id native_swarm_lane_diagnostics until the separate naming worktree provides an alias. It MUST register a real SwarmLaneDiagnostics pane/factory instead of placeholder fallback and expose stable author_id targets for run rows, lane rows, message rows, filters, deep links, payload/promotion drilldowns, and recovery actions.

Argus proof MUST use Rust native list_widgets/click_widget/set_value/screenshot or egui/AccessKit kittest evidence to open diagnostics, filter by run_id/lane_id/message_id, inspect one payload/promotion drilldown, capture screenshot or AccessKit tree, and compare displayed counts/refs against backend canonical state.

Negative proof MUST fail when Argus cannot see the pane, author_id is missing/unstable, backend canonical counts do not match rendered rows, or FR-only diagnostic posture omits internal_diagnostics/Palmistry tier status.

After implementation and before READY_FOR_VALIDATION, this MT MUST receive post-implementation adversarial review against the actual diff. Findings must be fixed or converted into explicit lifecycle blocker/missing_resource/proof_status state; the implementer may not self-certify COMPLETED.
scope.risk_if_missed: The operator still cannot see how cloud and local models are coordinating.

implementation_notes: Naming or renaming the internal viewing tool is out of scope for this WP; use the project-approved name from the separate viewing-tool worktree/session once settled.

The project-named internal viewing tool must render ModelLaneRun topology, lane cards/rows, status, last activity, message flow, payload refs, tool calls, artifacts, validation, promotion state, runtime errors, and recovery hints.
It must read backend TraceProjection/durable state rather than using rendered-row count as truth.
UI work must remain quiet and model-operable through stable selectors/IPC/backend navigation; no OS keyboard/cursor automation.
Visual proof must include normal, constrained, and edge-state captures where UI is touched, plus renderer/runtime error scans.
AutoGen Studio research supports visible agent actions, messages, metrics, debugging, and evaluation; Handshake must render these from durable TraceProjection/Flight Recorder/EventLedger state.
UI counts, rendered rows, filtered lists, and cached projections are not proof of canonical lane state; debug views must surface source/freshness/canonical refs.
Stable selectors, route/query params, and backend lookup IDs are required so future model operators can inspect state without fragile screen reading.
Use Flight Recorder/EventLedger payloads as bounded events plus artifact refs; do not put unbounded model output, raw secrets, or prompt-only state in event payloads.

If this MT changes any product behavior, storage/event contract, command, IPC/API path, diagnostic surface, model lane, or navigation path, update the in-product UserManual in the same implementation change per HBR-MAN-004.

Product implementation for this MT must be Rust-only: Rust backend plus Rust native egui/Argus surfaces where UI is touched. Legacy app/src, app/src-tauri, React, Tauri/WebView, TypeScript, JavaScript, docs/model-manual, and npm proof targets are reference-only or forbidden and MUST NOT satisfy implementation/proof authority.

Proof commands must name exact Rust test targets or exact test names. Zero matching tests is a hard failure. Deterministic provider fakes are allowed for cloud/local model calls, but PostgreSQL/EventLedger/ArtifactStore/CRDT/UserManual/Argus authority must touch real Handshake-owned Rust resources or an explicit real PostgreSQL URL.
Each behavior-changing MT must include same-change UserManual coverage for the changed behavior; MT-011 audits coverage and cannot substitute for same-change HBR-MAN-004 proof.

This pre-implementation contract review does not replace post-implementation review. After coding, run adversarial review against the actual diff through the listed lenses and update MT status signaling before handoff.

risks: A UI trace can show only rendered subset while backend state differs.
minimum_controls: Verify UI against canonical backend state and use visual layout checks.

research_basis.authority_posture: Master spec/SPEC_CURRENT/product code are authority; HANDSHAKE_PRODUCT_REFERENCE.md and external research are navigation/evidence only.
product_code_evidence: .GOV/spec/master-spec-v02.197/spec-modules/04-llm-infrastructure.md:1544 leaves internal viewing-tool naming to another worktree
src/frontend/handshake_native/ contains canonical native frontend surfaces
app/src legacy React/Tauri app is reference-only for this WP
HBR-VIS-004 requires TraceProjection inspection for non-UI behavior
patterns_applied: Typed multi-agent orchestration becomes ModelLaneRun/ModelLane/ModelLaneMessage records, never peer-to-peer chat authority.
Durable workflow/replay patterns become PostgreSQL/EventLedger aggregates, checkpoints, projections, and deterministic state transitions.
CRDT research becomes explicit draft/proposal/update/state-vector/snapshot/stale-base records before promotion.
Trace/span research becomes propagated trace_id/span_id/model_session_id/lane_id correlation across lanes, tools, providers, artifacts, and diagnostics.
Tool protocol research becomes Handshake capability check, consent/projection, Flight Recorder/EventLedger logging, execution, result capture, and recovery.
rejected_options: peer-to-peer model channels outside coordinator-owned ModelLaneMessage records
legacy app/src React/Tauri code as runtime authority
SQLite runtime/test/fixture/cache/fallback authority for this WP
Flight Recorder-only replay without PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority rows
manual or markdown prose as product runtime authority
research_basis.selected_handshake_translation: Diagnostics must be native/debug-surface or backend TraceProjection first, with stable model-operable IDs, trace/span keys, Locus/Loom/FEMS/ContextBundle refs, and no legacy app/src authority.

handshake_compatibility.product_reference_file_posture: reference/navigation only; do not override SPEC_CURRENT, master spec, build rules, or product code evidence
handshake_compatibility.proposal_promotion_model: Models may propose, critique, summarize, and produce artifacts; Handshake validates/promotes deterministically through Rust backend authority surfaces before state becomes authoritative.
durable_authority: CRDT/write-box when shared editable state is touched
observability_required: HBR-INT-009 tier posture for Flight Recorder, internal_diagnostics, Palmistry
handshake_compatibility.no_context_model_requirement: A model with no chat history must be able to implement and verify this MT from this contract, SPEC_CURRENT/master spec, product code paths, UserManual/backend navigation, and listed proof targets.
handshake_compatibility.rust_only_implementation: Rust backend plus Rust native egui/Argus only. TypeScript/React/app/src/app-src-tauri/npm/docs-model-manual are not implementation or proof authority for this WP.
handshake_compatibility.spec_realism_gate: Implementer may move only to READY_FOR_VALIDATION after exact Rust proof targets touch the named real resources; COMPLETED is validator-only.
